channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden admin slapped with major lawsuit over alleged refusal to help state purge noncitizens from voter rolls
item.description: Florida is suing the Biden administration over what it alleges is a refusal by the Department of Homeland Security to aid in its efforts to remove noncitizens from the voter rolls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris barnstorms Wisconsin in 1-day swing state tour targeting young voters
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is barnstorming in swing state Wisconsin on Thursday with only weeks to go until the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Biden agency issues blunt message to criminal immigrants after explosive data becomes liability for admin
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement are issuing a tough warning to noncitizens who commit felonies, telling them they forfeit the privilege to be in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas’ order to ask hospital patients’ citizenship status renews focus on the state’s large uninsured population
item.description: On Nov. 1, hospitals will begin asking patients their citizenship status. But data suggests uninsured citizens, not immigrants, cost Texas hospitals more.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Terri Langford, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other service workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell called Trump ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ in private after the 2020 election, a new book says
item.description: Senate Republican leader Mitch McConnell said after the 2020 election that then-President Donald Trump was “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and a “despicable human being.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Epic clash: Pressed by Fox News, Kamala Harris comes out swinging
item.description: In her first interview on Fox News, Kamala Harris ducked questions, raised her voice and appeared to be trying to project an image of standing up to both Trump and the network.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As women seek open congressional seats in Maryland, reproductive rights are front and center
item.description: In an election where the future of reproductive rights is on the ballot in Maryland and elsewhere across the country, the state’s all-male congressional delegation stands to gain an influx of women.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In the campaign's final weeks, Harris is keeping a focus on 'blue wall' states
item.description: Kamala Harris is spending her week trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Harris' interview with Fox News is marked by testy exchanges over immigration and more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris engaged in a combative interview with Fox News on Wednesday, sparring with anchor Bret Baier on immigration and shifting policy positions while asserting that if elected, she would not represent a continuation of Joe Biden's presidency.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Kern County voters say ballots are missing; election officials say it's not a concern
item.description: Once your ballot arrives in the mail, you can fill it out and then send it back by mail or drop it off at a drop box.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DENIED: How one Tampa Bay couple recovers after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Floridians across the coast and beyond are feeling overwhelmed with the FEMA application process and denial while struggling on the road to recovery, calling this a “nightmare” situation.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ag leaders voice concerns over negative impacts of tariffs proposed in presidential election
item.description: Midwestern agricultural leaders say they are concerned new tariffs being suggested by former President Donald Trump as part of his reelection bid could result in a damaging trade war. They say it could cost the corn and soybeans billions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Harris interview with Fox News showcases a change in strategy for Democrats with network
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Kamala Harris ' interview with Fox News Channel's Bret Baier on Wednesday is the latest indication that Democrats during this campaign are increasingly willing to engage with a network well-stocked with supporters of opponent Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is California's Proposition 5 on votes for bond measures?
item.description: Nearly every election cycle, Californians vote on a measures to allow local governments to borrow money in the form of bonds. Under state law, however, it is quite hard for these measures to actually pass — something that could change in November.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US to provide Ukraine with another $425M worth of supplies to help defend itself against Russia
item.description: The U.S. announced it would be sending a $425 million aid package to Ukraine that includes surface-to-air missiles and more to defend itself against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk commits $70 million to boost Donald Trump
item.description: Elon Musk plunged more than $70 million into helping Donald Trump and other Republicans win in November's election, making him one of the biggest donors to GOP causes this campaign season, according to campaign finance disclosures released this week.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Striking Boeing workers boo after Democratic Sen. Maria Cantwell criticizes Trump
item.description: Sen. Maria Cantwell, D-Wash., was booed after mentioning a housing shortage and blaming it on former President Trump during a union rally with striking Boeing workers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump spends big on anti-trans ads for 2024 election
item.description: Trump spends more on ads about transgender women and girls in sports and gender transitions than on Bidenomics, fracking and taxes.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: The 3rd Precinct keeps showing up in the GOP playbook. Minneapolis wants to turn the page
item.description: Speaking outside the former 3rd Precinct in Minneapolis this week, Republican vice presidential candidate JD Vance criticized Gov. Tim Walz for his response to civil unrest in 2020, painting a picture of a burned-down city with rampant crime.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter, at age 100, casts his 2024 ballot by mail
item.description: His son Chip Carter said before the family gathering that his father had this election very much in mind.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs owner backs kicker Harrison Butker forming PAC supporting ‘traditional values’
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs owner Clark Hunt says he has no issue with kicker Harrison Butker forming a political action committee designed to encourage Christians to vote for “traditional values.”.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris leading Trump by 5 points among likely voters: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris is leading former President Trump by 5 points nationally among likely voters, according to a Marist Poll survey released Wednesday. Harris and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), received 52 percent support, while Trump and his running mate, Sen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump: Black and Hispanic voters who vote for Harris 'gotta have your head examined'
item.description: “Any African American or Hispanic– and you know how well I’m doing there – that votes for Kamala, you gotta have your head examined,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Harris outreach to Black men: New policy, interviews, meetings
item.description: On the economic front, Harris intends to provide one million loans that would be fully forgivable of up to $20,000 to Black entrepreneurs.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota city says Trump campaign still owes more than $200,000 for July rally
item.description: Local officials say former President Donald Trump’s campaign still owes a central Minnesota city nearly $209,000 for services related to a July campaign rally. The Minnesota Star Tribune reports that the bill is for services beyond what St. Cloud normally provides.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump rally arrest: Vem Miller says it's 'false allegations'
item.description: A self-proclaimed "kindergarten kid when it comes to gun," Miller claims the charges against him are all "false.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rebuilding Florida after Hurricane Milton and protecting against scams
item.description:
Recovery efforts can unite communities after catastrophic damage. Neighbors come together to help one another, while state officials and law enforcement work diligently to restore order. Gradually, residents begin to see their homes return to normalcy.
But even with the good, comes the bad.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Annette Dawson Owens discusses education, healthcare, and affordable housing
item.description: Annette Dawson Owens, a Republican candidate for Nevada’s Assembly District 29, recently discussed her views on education, healthcare, and affordable housing on 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News
